error.regex: (worker_connections are not enough|worker_connections.*not enough|accept\(\) failed.*Resource temporarily unavailable|too many connections.*worker)

verdict.summary: nginx worker process ran out of connection slots. Default 1024 is too low for high-traffic sites.

dead_ends.action: Set worker_connections to extremely high value like 1000000
dead_ends.why_fails: Each connection uses memory (~1-2KB). Unreachable high values waste kernel resources. Set based on actual peak + headroom.

dead_ends.action: Only increase worker_connections without checking ulimit
dead_ends.why_fails: OS file descriptor limit (ulimit -n) must be >= worker_connections. nginx cannot open more FDs than the OS allows.

workarounds.action: Increase worker_connections to match expected concurrency
workarounds.how: events { worker_connections 4096; }  # in nginx.conf; rule of thumb: 2-4x expected concurrent connections
In /etc/security/limits.conf: nginx soft nofile 65536; nginx hard nofile 65536; also: worker_rlimit_nofile 65536; in nginx.conf

workarounds.action: Enable multi_accept to handle multiple connections per event
workarounds.how: events { multi_accept on; use epoll; }  # accept all pending connections at once instead of one by one
